subject: ap-precalculus
title: Sinusoidal Modeling: Ferris Wheel & Passenger Height
stimulus: A Ferris wheel has a diameter of $60$ meters and its center is $35$ meters above the ground. The wheel completes one full revolution every $4$ minutes. A passenger boards at the lowest point of the wheel at time $t = 0$ minutes. The sinusoidal function $H$ models the height of the passenger above the ground, in meters, as a function of time $t$, in minutes.

parts.prompt:
The function $H$ can be written in the form $H(t) = a\sin(b(t + c)) + d$.

(i) Find the values of constants $a$, $b$, $c$, and $d$.

(ii) Find the height of the passenger at $t = 1$ minute. Show the computations that lead to your answer.
parts.scoring_notes: Multiple valid parameterizations exist. The canonical form is $H(t) = -30\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{2}(t+1)\right) + 35$. Also accept $H(t) = 30\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{2}(t-1)\right) + 35$ or $H(t) = -30\cos\left(\frac{\pi}{2}(t-2)\right) + 35$. The key constraints are: maximum height $= 65$ m, minimum height $= 5$ m, period $= 4$ min, starts at minimum at $t = 0$. Verify: $H(0) = -30\sin(\frac{\pi}{2}) + 35 = -30 + 35 = 5$ ✓. For part (ii), $H(1) = -30\sin(\frac{\pi}{2}(2)) + 35 = -30\sin(\pi) + 35 = 0 + 35 = 35$ m. If student uses an alternate valid form, full credit if the substitution is correct.

scoring_points.description: Correct parameter values — $a = -30$ (amplitude, negative because passenger starts at minimum), $d = 35$ (midline = center height), $b = \frac{\pi}{2}$ (period $= 4$ minutes, so $b = \frac{2\pi}{4} = \frac{\pi}{2}$), $c = 1$ (phase shift needed: $H(0) = -30\sin(\frac{\pi}{2}(0+1)) + 35 = -30\sin(\frac{\pi}{2}) + 35 = -30(1) + 35 = 5$ ✓, the minimum at $t = 0$); accept $a = 30$ with appropriate $c$ value (e.g., $c = -1$ for $H(t) = 30\sin(\frac{\pi}{2}(t-1)) + 35$)

required_elements: Correct parameter values — $a = -30$ (amplitude, negative because passenger starts at minimum), $d = 35$ (midline = center height), $b = \frac{\pi}{2}$ (period $= 4$ minutes, so $b = \frac{2\pi}{4} = \frac{\pi}{2}$), $c = 1$ (phase shift needed: $H(0) = -30\sin(\frac{\pi}{2}(0+1)) + 35 = -30\sin(\frac{\pi}{2}) + 35 = -30(1) + 35 = 5$ ✓, the minimum at $t = 0$); accept $a = 30$ with appropriate $c$ value (e.g., $c = -1$ for $H(t) = 30\sin(\frac{\pi}{2}(t-1)) + 35$)
alternatives.correct_example: Correct parameter values — $a = -30$ (amplitude, negative because passenger starts at minimum), $d = 35$ (midline = center height), $b = \frac{\pi}{2}$ (period $= 4$ minutes, so $b = \frac{2\pi}{4} = \frac{\pi}{2}$), $c = 1$ (phase shift needed: $H(0) = -30\sin(\frac{\pi}{2}(0+1)) + 35 = -30\sin(\frac{\pi}{2}) + 35 = -30(1) + 35 = 5$ ✓, the minimum at $t = 0$); accept $a = 30$ with appropriate $c$ value (e.g., $c = -1$ for $H(t) = 30\sin(\frac{\pi}{2}(t-1)) + 35$)

scoring_points.description: Correct height $H(1) = -30\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{2}(1+1)\right) + 35 = -30\sin(\pi) + 35 = -30(0) + 35 = 35$ meters; supporting work must show substitution of $t = 1$

required_elements: Correct height $H(1) = -30\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{2}(1+1)\right) + 35 = -30\sin(\pi) + 35 = -30(0) + 35 = 35$ meters; supporting work must show substitution of $t = 1$
alternatives.correct_example: Correct height $H(1) = -30\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{2}(1+1)\right) + 35 = -30\sin(\pi) + 35 = -30(0) + 35 = 35$ meters; supporting work must show substitution of $t = 1$

parts.prompt:
A passenger boards the Ferris wheel at the lowest point at $t = 0$ and rides for a full $12$ minutes (three complete revolutions).

(i) Find all values of $t$ in $0 < t < 12$ at which the passenger is at a height of exactly $35$ meters (the center height). Express your answers as exact values.

(ii) On the interval $0 < t < 1$, is the rate of change of $H$ positive or negative? Justify your answer using the graph of $H$.
parts.scoring_notes: For part (i), the midline of $H$ is $35$ m. Using $H(t) = -30\sin(\frac{\pi}{2}(t+1)) + 35$, setting $H = 35$ gives $\sin(\frac{\pi}{2}(t+1)) = 0$, so $t+1 = 2k$ for integer $k$; $t = 2k - 1$. Within $(0, 12)$: $t = 1, 3, 5, 7, 9, 11$. All six must be listed. For part (ii), the justification must connect the passenger's upward movement (from minimum to midline) to the positive rate of change. A response that correctly states 'positive' and references the increasing height on $(0,1)$ earns the point. No calculus required.

scoring_points.description: Correct times — setting $H(t) = 35$: $-30\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{2}(t+1)\right) + 35 = 35 \Rightarrow \sin\left(\frac{\pi}{2}(t+1)\right) = 0 \Rightarrow \frac{\pi}{2}(t+1) = k\pi$ for integer $k$; so $t + 1 = 2k$, i.e., $t = 2k - 1$; solutions in $(0, 12)$ are $t = 1, 3, 5, 7, 9, 11$; all six values required with no incorrect extras

required_elements: Correct times — setting $H(t) = 35$: $-30\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{2}(t+1)\right) + 35 = 35 \Rightarrow \sin\left(\frac{\pi}{2}(t+1)\right) = 0 \Rightarrow \frac{\pi}{2}(t+1) = k\pi$ for integer $k$; so $t + 1 = 2k$, i.e., $t = 2k - 1$; solutions in $(0, 12)$ are $t = 1, 3, 5, 7, 9, 11$; all six values required with no incorrect extras
alternatives.correct_example: Correct times — setting $H(t) = 35$: $-30\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{2}(t+1)\right) + 35 = 35 \Rightarrow \sin\left(\frac{\pi}{2}(t+1)\right) = 0 \Rightarrow \frac{\pi}{2}(t+1) = k\pi$ for integer $k$; so $t + 1 = 2k$, i.e., $t = 2k - 1$; solutions in $(0, 12)$ are $t = 1, 3, 5, 7, 9, 11$; all six values required with no incorrect extras

scoring_points.description: Correct answer 'positive' with justification — on $(0, 1)$ the passenger rises from the minimum height of $5$ m at $t = 0$ to the midline height of $35$ m at $t = 1$, so $H$ is increasing and its rate of change is positive on this interval

required_elements: Correct answer 'positive' with justification — on $(0, 1)$ the passenger rises from the minimum height of $5$ m at $t = 0$ to the midline height of $35$ m at $t = 1$, so $H$ is increasing and its rate of change is positive on this interval
alternatives.correct_example: Correct answer 'positive' with justification — on $(0, 1)$ the passenger rises from the minimum height of $5$ m at $t = 0$ to the midline height of $35$ m at $t = 1$, so $H$ is increasing and its rate of change is positive on this interval

parts.prompt:
(i) On the interval $0 < t < 2$, which of the following is true about $H$?
a. $H$ is positive and increasing.
b. $H$ is positive and decreasing.
c. $H$ is negative and increasing.
d. $H$ is negative and decreasing.

(ii) A second Ferris wheel at the same amusement park has a function $H_2(t) = 20\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{3}(t - 1.5)\right) + 40$ modeling passenger height, in meters. Find the first positive time $t$ at which both passengers are at the same height. Show the computations that lead to your answer, or indicate if no such $t$ exists on $0 < t \leq 6$.
parts.scoring_notes: For part (i), if both letter and written description are given, the written description is scored. For part (ii), the equation $H(t) = H_2(t)$ must be set up correctly using $H(t) = -30\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{2}(t+1)\right) + 35$. At $t = 0$: $H = 5$ m and $H_2 = 20\sin(-\frac{\pi}{2}) + 40 = 20$ m (so $H < H_2$); at $t = 1$: $H = 35$ m and $H_2 = 20\sin(-\frac{\pi}{6}) + 40 = 30$ m (so $H > H_2$). The crossing occurs on $(0, 1)$ near $t \approx 0.85$. An exact closed-form solution does not exist; a numerical or graphical method is required. The first positive intersection near $t \approx 0.85$ earns the point if the setup is shown. Accept any value in $[0.80, 0.90]$ obtained with a valid setup. Numerical methods are expected; accept any approach that correctly equates the two height functions and solves graphically or numerically.

scoring_points.description: Correct answer — choice (a): $H$ is positive and increasing on $(0, 2)$ (the passenger rises from the minimum of $5$ m at $t = 0$ through the midline of $35$ m at $t = 1$ to the maximum of $65$ m at $t = 2$; all heights are positive and the function is increasing throughout this interval)

required_elements: Correct answer — choice (a): $H$ is positive and increasing on $(0, 2)$ (the passenger rises from the minimum of $5$ m at $t = 0$ through the midline of $35$ m at $t = 1$ to the maximum of $65$ m at $t = 2$; all heights are positive and the function is increasing throughout this interval)
alternatives.correct_example: Correct answer — choice (a): $H$ is positive and increasing on $(0, 2)$ (the passenger rises from the minimum of $5$ m at $t = 0$ through the midline of $35$ m at $t = 1$ to the maximum of $65$ m at $t = 2$; all heights are positive and the function is increasing throughout this interval)

scoring_points.description: Correct first time — setting $-30\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{2}(t+1)\right) + 35 = 20\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{3}(t-1.5)\right) + 40$ and solving numerically or by inspection; accept $t \approx 0.85$ (from numerical/graphical approach); show the equal-height setup with both functions equated

required_elements: Correct first time — setting $-30\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{2}(t+1)\right) + 35 = 20\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{3}(t-1.5)\right) + 40$ and solving numerically or by inspection; accept $t \approx 0.85$ (from numerical/graphical approach); show the equal-height setup with both functions equated
